summary: Iteration 223 — Independently re-verified the batch FIFO auto-allocation fix in POST /api/distributors/{distributor_id}/promo-deliveries/{dispatch_id}/confirm. 3/3 pytest cases PASS (re-runnable / idempotent). Fresh qa-* seed data was created directly in Mongo and torn down at the end. (1) Happy path: a batch-less draft of qty=6 at a batch-tracked warehouse (QB1 qty 5 created 2026-01-01, QB2 qty 3 created 2026-02-01) confirms HTTP 200, status flips to 'confirmed', and distributor_delivery_items is rewritten into 2 FIFO-allocated lines QB1 qty 5 line_value 500 + QB2 qty 1 line_value 100 (each with batch_id + batch_code set). (2) Reservation-aware shortfall: a second qty=5 batch-less draft against the SAME warehouse returns HTTP 400 with detail containing 'need 5' and 'available 2' (NOT 'available 0') because the previously confirmed promo reserves 6 of the 8 total batched units; the failing draft's line item is left untouched (still batch-less) and the delivery remains 'draft'. (3) Regression: a draft on a non-batch-tracked location (track_batches=false) with qty=3 against a batch-less stock row of qty 10 confirms HTTP 200 and the line item is NOT rewritten (no batch_id added). All qa-* test data cleaned up — DB confirmed empty post-run.
critical_code_review_comments: promo_dispatch.py is 1014 lines — already large. _allocate_batches_if_needed is well-scoped but the file would benefit from extracting (a) Zoho push helpers, (b) confirm/complete/cancel actions, and (c) the new allocator + reservation helpers into separate modules to stay under 700 lines.

_allocate_batches_if_needed sorts only on production_date|created_at|'9999' fallback — if both are missing on a batch row it lands at end-of-list which is a reasonable default, but consider also persisting created_at via the seed/import paths so FIFO is deterministic in older tenants.

Per-line `await db.distributor_delivery_items.insert_one` in the rewrite loop (promo_dispatch.py ~278) — switch to insert_many for atomicity and fewer round trips; otherwise a mid-loop failure leaves a half-rewritten delivery (only the delete_many is committed).

The rewrite happens BEFORE the reservation-aware validation that follows; if validation later raises (e.g. derived on-hand mismatch on a non-batch source), the items are already rewritten and the delivery stays 'draft' with allocated batch lines. Functionally OK for the batch-tracked path (validation can only fail if allocator already raised), but worth a unit test pinning the invariant.

Reservation excludes only the current dispatch_id; if a same-tenant operator races two confirms on different drafts there is no lock — last writer wins and may double-reserve. Consider a per-(distributor_id, location_id) advisory lock for concurrent confirms.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_promo_confirm_batch_alloc.py (new — 3 pytest cases + module-level setup/teardown using motor)
seed_data_creation: All transient: 1 distributor (qa-dist-1) + 3 locations (qa-factory-1 is_factory, qa-wh-1 track_batches=true, qa-wh-2 track_batches=false) + 3 distributor_stock rows (2 batched QB1/QB2 at qa-wh-1, 1 plain at qa-wh-2) + 3 distributor_deliveries (1 confirmed + 1 draft batch-less + 1 confirmed plain) + their items. Module-scoped autouse fixture cleans EVERYTHING with id starting qa-* at session start AND end — verified empty post-run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Tests are idempotent and self-cleaning. Re-run: `pytest /app/backend/tests/test_promo_confirm_batch_alloc.py -v`. JUnit XML at /app/test_reports/pytest/iteration_223.xml. The 3 tests use module-scoped fixtures (setup runs once, teardown wipes id={qa-dist-1, qa-wh-1, qa-wh-2, qa-factory-1} and all child stock + deliveries + items). Tests rely on master_skus having at least one document with id+sku_name (any tenant); current preview has 51cbec53-a1d5-4e23-b708-a1fd0c014059 '20L Premium'. Login is httpOnly cookie via requests.Session() — no token in body.
rca of the issue: Bug RCA (already fixed by main agent; this iteration re-verified): On a batch-tracked warehouse, the validation loop in confirm_promo_dispatch keyed by (sku_id, batch_id), and for a DO-originated draft batch_id was None. The Mongo lookup `{'batch_id': {'$in': [None]}}` returned zero matching rows (a batch-tracked warehouse has no null-batch stock), so the code falsely reported 'available 0' even though plenty of batched stock existed. Fix (verified): _allocate_batches_if_needed runs BEFORE validation whenever `loc.track_batches and any(not it.get('batch_id') for it in items)`. It fetches all positive-quantity batched rows for the SKU, sorts FIFO by production_date|created_at, subtracts the reservation map (which now excludes the current dispatch), greedily allocates and splits the line, then rewrites distributor_delivery_items via delete_many + insert_one per allocation. The subsequent validation now finds the (sku, batch) keys and passes. On shortfall, the allocator itself raises HTTP 400 with 'need X, available Y' computed from the truly available batched total (post-reservation), matching the contract. Reproduced both happy & shortfall paths independently with fresh qa-* data.
